title: OSMM Measurement Framework Object
description: Work Product that operationalizes a Marketing Strategy's objectives into measurable KPIs: the tiered metric set, each metric's definition and target, and the cadence and approach for reporting over a planning horizon. The framework is the plan (what will be measured and the targets); actuals are recorded by the Phase 7 Performance Measurement Object. Canonical schema for object_type 'measurement_framework'. The osmm-measurement-framework-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

measurement_framework_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'MEF-' + lowercase hyphen slug, scoped to business plus horizon. Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

name.description: The framework's readable label, usually entity + horizon (e.g. "IBM 2026 Measurement Framework").

linked_business_context.description: Id of the owning Business Context. Use 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists.

linked_marketing_strategy.description: Id of the Marketing Strategy this framework measures. Use 'MKS-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists. Realizes the inverse of the strategy's linked_measurement_framework.

time_horizon.description: The period measured; should match the linked strategy's time_horizon.

north_star_metric.description: The single most important measure for the horizon, if there is one. Optional.

metrics.description: The KPI set (typically 3-8 metrics). Each carries a name, tier, and definition.

tier.description: Governed tier: 'primary' (headline KPIs reported to leadership), 'supporting' (diagnostic metrics), or 'guardrail' (health metrics that must not degrade).

definition.description: What the metric counts / how it is calculated — unambiguous enough that two analysts count it the same.

supports_objective.description: The strategy objective this metric measures (text echo of the strategy's wording). Optional.

cadence.description: Measurement frequency (e.g. monthly, quarterly, per-campaign). Optional.

data_source.description: Where the data comes from. Optional.

measurement_cadence.description: Overall reporting/review rhythm (e.g. "Monthly business review; quarterly strategic review"). Optional.

measurement_approach.description: Notes on attribution method or measurement design. Optional.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date.
